notes: Two real committed decks, one per skill branch. textlayer-kowa has a genuine PDF text layer (108 fonts, ToUnicode CMaps) so the cheap stdlib scripts/extract_deck_text.py recovers it (exit 0) — here the skill's win over a baseline is COST (free script vs reading 21 slide PNGs as vision). vision-khew is an image-only deck (DCTDecode JPEGs, zero fonts) — the script returns nothing (exit 1), so the skill must fall back to a one-time vision pass over the slides/ PNGs; here both with- and without-skill must use vision, so the discriminator is the OUTPUT CONTRACT (a slides.txt sibling, ## slide-NN sections one per slide, exact-casing term preservation), not cost. (The third talk deck, Soh, is also image-only and would be a redundant second vision case; left out to keep iteration-1 cheap.) Graded deterministically by grade.py against the staged inputs: section count == staged PNG count, structure/encoding invariants, and per-term verbatim (case-sensitive) presence. The committed slides.txt in each talk folder is the human-verified oracle the curated term lists were drawn from; prepare.py never stages it (the work dir gets only slides.pdf + slides/). HARNESS CONTRACT: (1) run `python3 prepare.py runs/<iteration>` BEFORE spawning executors — prompts carry {WORK_DIR}, resolved into each run's eval_metadata.json and the printed run plan; (2) the skill IS installed in this workspace, so without_skill executors must be told to ignore the installed slides-pdf-to-text skill and extract the deck text however they would unaided (baseline-contamination guard); (3) after each executor finishes, write its token/duration notification to <run-dir>/timing.json — outputs are graded in place under <run-dir>/work/. with_skill executors run the skill, which writes work/slides.txt.

evals.name: textlayer-kowa
evals.prompt: I have a talk's slide deck at {WORK_DIR}/slides.pdf, with the rendered slide images in {WORK_DIR}/slides/. Please pull the deck's text out into a single reusable plain-text file beside the PDF, so later steps can build a term glossary from it without re-reading the slide images. Keep the exact spelling and casing of product, tool, and tech names.
evals.expected_output: A slides.txt written beside slides.pdf with one `## slide-NN` section per slide in order (21 slides), every section non-empty, UTF-8 without a BOM. This deck carries a real PDF text layer, so the cheap stdlib extractor recovers it (the skill should NOT need a vision pass). Product/tech names appear verbatim with exact casing: ChatGPT, Cursor, Claude Code, Ruby on Rails, Vue JS, Payboy, HRMS, StackOverflow. The source slides.pdf and the slides/ PNGs are left untouched.
assertions: slides.txt exists beside the source slides.pdf
slides.txt parses into ## slide-NN sections, one per slide (count == staged PNG count)
Section headers are zero-padded, 1-based, contiguous: slide-01..slide-NN
No empty sections in slides.txt
slides.txt is UTF-8 without a BOM
The term 'ChatGPT' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Cursor' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Claude Code' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Ruby on Rails' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Vue JS' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Payboy' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'HRMS' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'StackOverflow' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The source slides.pdf is byte-identical to the fixture (sha256 unchanged)
The slide PNGs under slides/ are untouched (sha256 unchanged)

evals.name: vision-khew
evals.prompt: I have a talk's slide deck at {WORK_DIR}/slides.pdf, with the rendered slide images in {WORK_DIR}/slides/. Please pull the deck's text out into a single reusable plain-text file beside the PDF, so later steps can build a term glossary from it without re-reading the slide images. Keep the exact spelling and casing of product, tool, and tech names.
evals.expected_output: A slides.txt written beside slides.pdf with one `## slide-NN` section per slide in order (6 slides), every section non-empty, UTF-8 without a BOM. This deck is image-only (no PDF text layer), so the cheap extractor returns nothing and the skill must transcribe the slides/ PNGs via a one-time vision pass. Tech names appear verbatim with exact casing: IaC, Infrastructure as Code, Terraform, Bicep, Ansible, Markdown. The source slides.pdf and the slides/ PNGs are left untouched.
assertions: slides.txt exists beside the source slides.pdf
slides.txt parses into ## slide-NN sections, one per slide (count == staged PNG count)
Section headers are zero-padded, 1-based, contiguous: slide-01..slide-NN
No empty sections in slides.txt
slides.txt is UTF-8 without a BOM
The term 'IaC' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Infrastructure as Code' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Terraform' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Bicep' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Ansible' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The term 'Markdown' is preserved verbatim (exact casing)
The source slides.pdf is byte-identical to the fixture (sha256 unchanged)
The slide PNGs under slides/ are untouched (sha256 unchanged)
